5:["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m9 18 6-6-6-6","key":"mthhwq"}]],"className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}]
6:["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog","className":"hover:text-orange-500 transition-colors","children":"Blog"}]
7:["$","li","2",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$5:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Workspace","className":"hover:text-orange-500 transition-colors","children":"Workspace"}]]}]
8:["$","li","3",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$5:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-stone-600 font-medium line-clamp-1","children":"Home Office Organization: 10 Systems That Keep Your Workspace Under Control"}]]}]
9:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Home Office Organization: 10 Systems That Keep Your Workspace Under Control"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/mia-collins","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1494790108377-be9c29b29330?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Mia Collins","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Mia Collins"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Workspace Designer"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","7 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"December 6, 2025"}]]}]]}]
a:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"An organised home office is not about tidiness for its own sake — it is about reducing the friction between you and your work. These are the systems that prevent entropy from taking over."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-organisation-problem","children":"The Organisation Problem"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A home office becomes disorganised not through negligence but through the absence of systems. When there is no defined place for something, it ends up on the desk. When there is no process for handling paper, it accumulates. When cables are not managed, they multiply."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Organisation is not a personality trait — it is an infrastructure question. Build the right infrastructure and the space stays ordered with minimal ongoing effort."}}],["$","h2","3",{"id":"1-the-single-capture-point","children":"1. The Single Capture Point"}],["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Every piece of incoming information — a receipt, a note, a task someone mentions in passing — needs a single defined destination. Without it, things end up on the desk, on the floor, in pockets, and eventually lost."}}],["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The system</strong>: one physical inbox tray on the desk. Everything incoming goes in here immediately. Process it daily. Nothing lives in the inbox — it is a temporary holding point only."}}],["$","h2","6",{"id":"2-the-two-folder-paper-system","children":"2. The Two-Folder Paper System"}],["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Paper is the primary source of desk clutter for most home office workers. The minimum viable paper system: <strong>In</strong> (to process today) and <strong>Archive</strong> (processed and kept). No other categories. No \"maybe\" pile."}}],["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Process the In folder daily. File or discard everything in it. Archive weekly. After three months, audit the Archive and discard anything you have not referenced. Most people find 80%+ of archived paper was never needed."}}],["$","h2","9",{"id":"3-the-daily-desk-reset","children":"3. The Daily Desk Reset"}],["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"At the end of every workday, return the desk to its baseline state. Everything off the desk that does not belong. File the papers. Cap the pens. Close the notebooks."}}],["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This takes three minutes. It creates a clean start the next morning and — more importantly — it provides a clear psychological boundary between work and rest. A desk that looks like work in progress makes it harder to mentally leave work."}}],["$","h2","12",{"id":"4-drawer-organisation","children":"4. Drawer Organisation"}],["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"An unorganised drawer becomes a black hole. Everything goes in, nothing comes out cleanly."}}],["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The rule</strong>: one category per drawer. Top drawer: active supplies (pens, sticky notes, paper clips). Middle drawer: reference documents currently in use. Bottom drawer: archive."}}],["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Use drawer dividers or small boxes to create fixed zones within each drawer. Once you have a fixed place for everything, returning things to their place takes no thought."}}],"$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e"]}],"$L1f","$L20","$L21"]}],"$L22"]}]
b:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
c:["$","$L23",null,{"children":["$","$24",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@25"}]}]
d:["$","h2","16",{"id":"5-cable-management-first","children":"5. Cable Management First"}]
e:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Visible cables create the impression of disorganisation regardless of everything else. Cables that are routed and hidden make the same desk look professional and intentional."}}]
f:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This is a one-time project. See our <a href=\"/blog/cable-management-desk-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">cable management guide</a> for the exact process."}}]
10:["$","h2","19",{"id":"6-the-vertical-storage-rule","children":"6. The Vertical Storage Rule"}]
11:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"In a home office, floor space is more valuable than wall space. Use walls, not floors:"}}]
12:["$","ul","21",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Floating shelves above the desk for reference books and notebooks"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Pegboards for tools, accessories, and cables"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Wall-mounted file holders instead of floor-standing file cabinets"}}]]}]
13:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Anything stored on the floor other than furniture creates visual clutter and collects dust."}}]
14:["$","h2","23",{"id":"7-weekly-reset-ritual","children":"7. Weekly Reset Ritual"}]
15:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Once per week — Friday afternoon or Sunday evening — do a full desk audit:"}}]
16:["$","ul","25",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Return everything that drifted to the desk to its proper home"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Process any inbox items that accumulated"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Review next week's commitments and lay out any materials needed"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Wipe the desk surface"}}]]}]
17:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This weekly reset prevents the slow accumulation that turns a functional workspace into a chaotic one. It takes 15–20 minutes and pays off all week."}}]
18:["$","h2","27",{"id":"8-digital-equals-physical","children":"8. Digital Equals Physical"}]
19:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A messy desktop (the computer one) impairs focus as surely as a messy physical desk. Apply the same rules: a desktop with fewer than 10 items, files saved to named folders rather than the desktop, a browser with bookmarks organised and the bar uncluttered."}}]
1a:["$","h2","29",{"id":"9-supplies-inventory","children":"9. Supplies Inventory"}]
1b:["$","p","30",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Over-buying supplies creates clutter. Under-buying creates friction. The minimum viable supplies for most home offices: 10 pens (you will lose 7), a pad of A4 paper, sticky notes, paper clips, and one stapler. Everything else is optional."}}]
1c:["$","h2","31",{"id":"10-the-rule-of-threes","children":"10. The Rule of Threes"}]
1d:["$","p","32",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For any category of item on or near the desk, own a maximum of three. Three pens. Three notebooks. Three chargers. The fourth one goes to a drawer or the cupboard."}}]
1e:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The constraint forces decisions about what you actually use and prevents the slow accumulation of things you keep because discarding them feels wasteful."}}]
1f:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium 20:["$","$L26",null,{"title":"Home Office Organization: 10 Systems That Keep Your Workspace Under Control","slug":"home-office-organization-tips"}]
